checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. All text content in the JSON has a clear basis in the source PDF slides. AI-generated elements like table summaries and image alt text are within acceptable limits.

I have verified every URL, social media handle, and email address in the output against the source. There are no fabricated items. While some contact information from page 19 is omitted (which is handled under Check C5), nothing in the output is invented.

All pages of the 27-page document are structurally represented in the JSON sections. Although content from certain pages (e.g., pages 7 and 8) is omitted, the sections they belong to are present. No entire page or section was silently dropped without trace.

Two tables in the document have major row omissions. The 'Broadband Speeds' table on page 3 of the source has 15 data rows, but the output version contains only 2. The 'Typical Broadband Usage' table on page 4 has 4 data rows in the source, but only 1 in the output. Both cases exceed the 20% threshold for a failure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3 table has 15 rows: General Browsing, Streaming Online Radio, VoIP Calls, Student, Telecommuting, File Downloading, Social Media, Streaming SD, Streaming HD, Streaming Ultra HD, Standard Personal Video Call, HD Personal Video Call, HD Video Teleconferencing, Game Console, Online Multiplayer. Page 4 table has 4 rows: 1 user, 2 users, 3 users, 4 users.
checks.b_evidence_output: The table under 'Broadband Speeds' has only 2 data rows: 'General Browsing and Email' and 'Streaming Ultra HD 4K Video'. The table under 'Typical Broadband Usage' has only 1 data row: '1 user on 1 device'.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are two significant omissions. First, page 19 of the source provides specific names, phone numbers, and emails for regional technical assistance contacts (Keith Conover, Jim Corrin, George T. Collier); these are entirely missing from the corresponding text in section 8. Second, the 'Adoption Graphics' section omits the progressive visual data/content shown on pages 7 and 8 of the source, only including the start and end states from pages 6 and 9.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 19 contains text for regional contacts: West (Keith Conover, 828-450-7933, Keith.Conover@nc.gov), Central (Jim Corrin, 919-353-1126, Jim.Corrin@nc.gov), East (George T. Collier, 919-754-6558, George.Collier@nc.gov). Pages 7 and 8 contain house icon grids showing 1/64 adoption and 64/64 adoption respectively.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON output for Section 8 (starting at reading_order 8) mentions the Technical Assistance Team but excludes all contact names, numbers, and emails. Section 6 (Adoption Graphics) includes only 2 images, omitting the visuals from pages 7 and 8.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 'Broadband Planning, First Steps....' (reading_order 8) and Section 'Adoption Graphics' (reading_order 6).

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Excluding the significant omissions previously noted, the remaining text accurately reflects the source material. Numerical values (e.g., speed ranges, dates, grant amounts) are correctly captured.

checks.a_analysis: The document contains sections with identical titles (e.g., 'Executive Order 91' appearing twice in Section 8), but this reflects the source document where pages 17 and 18 share the same title. No instances of accidental pipeline duplication (repeated paragraphs or list items) were found.

The heading hierarchy is generally maintained. Major section titles are treated as the primary headings, and subsections within Section 8 use Heading Level 3 (H3) correctly. While Section 8 merges several pages, the internal headings (Vision, Executive Order 91, etc.) correctly use a consistent sublevel.

The tables in Sections 3 and 4 are structurally corrupted. The 'Broadband Speeds' table on source page 3 contains over 15 activity rows, but the output only renders two data rows (General Browsing and Streaming Ultra HD 4K Video), omitting the vast majority of the table structure. Similarly, the 'Typical Broadband Usage' table on source page 4 contains rows for 1, 2, 3, and 4 users, but the output table only contains the row for 1 user, omitting the rest of the table's rows.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table on page 3 contains rows for Streaming Online Radio, VoIP Calls, Student, Telecommuting, File Downloading, Social Media, etc. Table on page 4 contains rows for 2, 3, and 4 users.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output table for 'Broadband Speeds' (RO 3) contains only 3 total rows (1 header, 2 data). The output table for 'Typical Broadband Usage' (RO 4) contains only 2 total rows (1 header, 1 data).

checks.a_analysis: The document's logical organization is severely compromised in Section 8. Content from 11 distinct slides (pages 11 through 21) is consolidated under a single section title ('2017 County Broadband Adoption Rates'). This breaks the information flow, as unrelated topics such as 'Executive Order 91', the 'Vision' statement, and 'The Playbook' website sections are grouped under an adoption rate map title from an earlier page.
checks.b_evidence_source: Pages 11 through 21 are distinct slides with their own titles and subjects (e.g., Vision, The Broadband Infrastructure Office, Executive Order 91).
checks.b_evidence_output: The entire block of content from pages 11-21 is contained within a single JSON section (reading_order 8) titled '2017 County Broadband Adoption Rates'.

checks.a_analysis: Lists are converted correctly. The complex nested list on page 2 (Broadband Technology Types) is faithfully represented with correct nesting in Section 2. The bullet points on pages 22, 23, 25, and 26 are also correctly identified and structured.

Most URLs and emails are correctly identified as interactive links. However, the URL 'www.ncbroadband.gov/sbp' found on source page 16 is rendered as plain text in Section 8 without an associated href attribute, while other similar URLs in the document are correctly linked.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization and flow of information present in the source PDF. The document's structure as a series of presentation slides is faithfully converted into a single-column web page layout. Sections appear in the correct chronological order, and the transition from graphical elements (like the hexagon infographics or chevron lists) to text-based lists and tables is done in a way that preserves the information hierarchy.

The table under the 'Typical Broadband Usage' section (corresponding to page 4 of the source PDF) is severely truncated. The source PDF contains four data rows (1, 2, 3, and 4 users/devices), whereas the HTML output only renders the first row. This results in the loss of 75% of the data in that table. Other tables, such as the 'Broadband Speeds' table and the 'GREAT Grant Program' list, are rendered correctly.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF on page 4 shows a table with four data rows: '1 user on 1 device', '2 users or devices', '3 users or devices', and '4 users or devices'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot for the 'Typical Broadband Usage' section shows a table containing only the header and the first data row: '1 user on 1 device 3-8 Mbps 3-8 Mbps 12-25 Mbps'.

checks.a_analysis: Multiple meaningful images and infographics are missing from the HTML output. These are replaced by green placeholder blocks. Most notably, the data-heavy maps on pages 5 ('Broadband Service Inventory'), 11 ('County Broadband Adoption Rates'), and 19 ('Technical Assistance Regions') are absent. Additionally, infographics on pages 10 and 12, the adoption graphics on pages 6-9, the 'Connecting North Carolina' graphic on page 16, and the report cover image on page 22 are missing. While some are decorative, the maps and report covers represent significant information content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 5 Map: 'North Carolina Broadband Service Inventory'; Page 11 Map: '2017 County Broadband Adoption Rates'; Page 22: 'The Homework Gap in North Carolina' report cover image.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output shows green placeholder boxes in place of the maps and graphics in the corresponding sections.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'North Carolina Broadband Service Inventory', 'Adoption Graphics', '2017 County Broadband Adoption Rates', and 'The Homework Gap in North Carolina'.

checks.a_analysis: No visual evidence of duplicated body content (paragraphs, tables, or sections) was found. Content blocks appear unique and follow the sequence established in the source document. The appearance of headings in the Table of Contents as well as the main body is expected accessible design.
